effectsurf 0.2.0.9000

Compare two surfaces side-by-side or as a difference surface

Source: R/surf-compare.R
surf_compare.Rd

Computes the difference between two effectsurf objects or creates a side-by-side comparison. Useful for comparing models (e.g., DAG-informed vs legacy), or comparing two different treatments.

Usage

surf_compare(
  object1,
  object2,
  type = c("difference", "side_by_side"),
  labels = list()
)

Arguments

object1

An effectsurf object.

object2

An effectsurf object with the same x and y variables and grid resolution.

type

Character. "difference" (default) computes object1 - object2 as a new surface. "side_by_side" returns both objects in a list for manual comparison.

labels

Named list. Custom labels for the difference surface.

Value

For "difference": a new effectsurf object. For "side_by_side": a named list with elements surface1 and surface2.

Examples

# \donttest{
if (requireNamespace("mgcv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  m1 <- mgcv::gam(mpg ~ s(wt) + s(hp), data = mtcars)
  m2 <- mgcv::gam(mpg ~ s(wt) + hp, data = mtcars)

  es1 <- surf_prediction(m1, x = "wt", y = "hp",
                         x_length = 20, y_length = 20)
  es2 <- surf_prediction(m2, x = "wt", y = "hp",
                         x_length = 20, y_length = 20)

  diff_surf <- surf_compare(es1, es2, type = "difference")
  plot(diff_surf)
}


# }
